data.body: EVAL-RUN INFRA BLOCKER (important): reviewing CONTAINER plans through the full gate OOM-kills the process on this machine. Root: plan_review/pass1.py:51 _CONTAINER_MAX_WORKERS=6 fans out 6 PARALLEL agentic child-reviews, each ~180s w/ large context (cache_read ~263K tokens, cumulative in ~2.6M over 37 steps); 6 concurrent large contexts exhaust RAM. E6 Exp-B died TWICE at the container fan-out over ticket 5fd2 (14 children) — once concurrent w/ E2 smoke, once ALONE. E6 sample has 4 containers (5fd2=14, b744=9, 4387=7, 8414=4 children) + 10 leaves. E2 CANNOT avoid containers (classes 2/3 ARE container defects: layer-cake + consumed-artifact-ordering) -> systemic. MITIGATION: harness-side runtime monkeypatch _CONTAINER_MAX_WORKERS=1 via wrapper /Users/joeoakhart/.claude/jobs/6717c52e/tmp/lowmem_run.py (REBAR_CONTAINER_WORKERS env; changes execution parallelism only, NOT verdicts) — bounds peak memory to 1 child context. COST: serial container review ~3min/child -> 14-child plan ~42min; E6 Exp-B ~6.5h serial, E2 many hours (40 container reviews), E3 huge. RESUMABLE via journals. Re-launched E6 run-b via lowmem wrapper (workers=1). E6 Exp-A already DONE (150 votes clean). Evals now run STRICTLY SEQUENTIAL (concurrent heavy evals also trigger the kill).
